title: MongoDB Change Stream CDC Flow
interaction_flow: MongoDbStreamingChangeEventSource → MongoDbConnections: create(rawConfig, dispatcher, partition) → MongoDbConnection
ChangeStreamPipelineFactory: create() combines sizePipeline + userAndInternalPipeline + splitPipeline (aggregation stages)

MongoUtils → MongoClient: client.watch(pipeline.getStages(), BsonDocument.class) [scope=DEPLOYMENT] OR client.getDatabase(db).watch() [scope=DATABASE] OR client.getDatabase(db).getCollection(coll).watch() [scope=COLLECTION] → ChangeStreamIterable<BsonDocument>
initChangeStream: IF captureMode.isFullUpdate() → stream.fullDocument(WHEN_AVAILABLE or UPDATE_LOOKUP)
MongoDbStreamingChangeEventSource → BufferingChangeStreamCursor: fromIterable(stream, taskContext, metrics, clock) → cursor
BufferingChangeStreamCursor: creates EventFetcher(stream, maxBatchSize, metrics, clock, pollInterval) + ExecutorService
EventFetcher: if document available → new ResumableChangeStreamEvent(document); else → new ResumableChangeStreamEvent(cursor.getResumeToken())
EventFetcher: enqueue(event) into ConcurrentLinkedQueue (semaphore-bounded by maxBatchSize)
BufferingChangeStreamCursor → EventFetcher: poll() from queue; updates lastResumeToken
IF resumableEvent.document.isPresent() → processChangeStreamDocument(doc, splitHandler, partition, offsetContext)
SplitEventHandler: if $changeStreamSplitLargeEvent fragments → collect and reassemble; else pass-through
SourceInfo: extract resumeToken = ResumeTokens.toBase64(event.getResumeToken()), ts = event.getClusterTime(), sessionTxnId
dispatchChangeEvent → new MongoDbChangeRecordEmitter(partition, offsetContext, clock, event, connectorConfig)
MongoDbChangeRecordEmitter: maps OperationType → Operation (INSERT→CREATE, UPDATE/REPLACE→UPDATE, DELETE→DELETE)
MongoDbChangeRecordEmitter → receiver: changeRecord(partition, schema, operation, key, value, offset, null)
MongoDbOffsetContext.getOffset() → returns map {TIMESTAMP, ORDER, RESUME_TOKEN, [LSID, TXN_NUMBER if txn], [INITIAL_SYNC if snapshot]}
resume_token_management: ResumeTokens.toBase64(BsonDocument) → BSON binary encode → Base64 string (stored in offset)
ResumeTokens.fromBase64(String) → Base64 decode → BsonDocument (used for stream.resumeAfter())
ResumeTokens.fromData(String) → wraps _data string as BsonDocument (old format fallback)
ResumeTokens.getTimestamp(BsonDocument) → extracts BsonTimestamp from canonical type 130 token bytes
SourceInfo.Position: {BsonTimestamp ts, SessionTransactionId changeStreamSessionTxnId, String resumeToken (base64)}
MongoDbOffsetContext stores RESUME_TOKEN key in Kafka offset map
MongoDbOffsetContext.Loader reads RESUME_TOKEN, TIMESTAMP, ORDER, LSID, TXN_NUMBER from stored offset
IT test shouldConsumeEventsFromOffsetWithDataResumeToken: verifies old string-format resume token is parsed and warns
capture_modes: CHANGE_STREAMS: basic oplog CDC via change streams
key_relationships: MongoDbStreamingChangeEventSource depends_on MongoDbConnectorConfig, EventDispatcher, ErrorHandler, MongoDbTaskContext, MongoDbStreamingChangeEventSourceMetrics
MongoDbStreamingChangeEventSource uses MongoUtils.openChangeStream() to get ChangeStreamIterable
MongoDbStreamingChangeEventSource uses BufferingChangeStreamCursor wrapping EventFetcher (background thread)
MongoDbOffsetContext extends CommonOffsetContext<SourceInfo>; delegates to SourceInfo for position/resumeToken tracking
SourceInfo.Position is immutable: {BsonTimestamp, SessionTransactionId, String resumeToken}
ChangeStreamPipelineFactory creates ChangeStreamPipeline with size/filter/split aggregation stages
BufferingChangeStreamCursor implements MongoChangeStreamCursor; contains EventFetcher (Runnable) + ConcurrentLinkedQueue + Semaphore
important_notes: The EventFetcher runs on a separate thread and pre-buffers events into a bounded ConcurrentLinkedQueue. Throttling (exponential backoff) activates after 5 consecutive empty polls. SplitEventHandler reassembles $changeStreamSplitLargeEvent fragments before dispatch. Resume token is stored as Base64-encoded BSON in the Kafka offset. On restart: if RESUME_TOKEN present → stream.resumeAfter(); else if timestamp → stream.startAtOperationTime(); else use config override. Pause/resume is supported via Semaphore + ReentrantLock Condition for incremental snapshot interleaving.
